  "findings": [
    "Weep holes are missing or blocked at the bottom course of the masonry veneer."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Rout out mortar joints at the bottom course to establish open weep holes at required intervals.",
    "Verify that flashing is undamaged and properly routed to the exterior behind the newly opened weeps."
  ],
  "rationale": "According to TMS 402 and BIA Technical Notes, weep holes must be provided in the outside wythe of masonry walls immediately above the flashing at maximum intervals of 33 inches for open head joints. Missing or blocked weep holes prevent trapped moisture from draining, leading to water damage, rot, and freeze-thaw spalling.",
  "references": [
    "TMS 402/602 Section 12.1.6.2",
    "BIA Technical Note 7",
    "IRC Section R703.8.6"
  ]
}
